findings.description: Header layout is reflowed on the first page.
findings.evidence: In the PDF, the agency officials' names appear as a right-aligned block at the top right, while in the HTML they are moved to a left-side stacked list under the logo. Main memo content remains intact.

findings.description: First-page heading and label styling differ from the source.
findings.evidence: The PDF shows an underlined serif 'MEMORANDUM' heading and traditional memo labels/alignment; the HTML uses a larger sans-serif heading with a horizontal rule and bold inline labels such as 'To:', 'From:', and 'Subject:'. Content is preserved but formatting is noticeably changed.

findings.description: Digital signature block is introduced in the HTML but not visible in the PDF rendering.
findings.evidence: Between the 'From' block and 'Subject' on page 1, the HTML includes 'Digitally signed by Elizabeth S Werner...' metadata, which is not present in the PDF page image. This alters visual formatting/readability, though likely reflects embedded signature metadata rather than lost content.

findings.description: Page 2 top-of-page header information is repositioned.
findings.evidence: In the PDF, the meeting report title/date/page number appear in a small header at the top right. In the HTML, this information is relocated lower on the page near the colored divider and left margin. Body section structure remains correct.

findings.description: Section heading typography differs on page 2.
findings.evidence: The PDF uses serif bold section numbering/title ('I. History/Background'); the HTML renders it as a larger sans-serif heading. Bullet list structure is preserved.

findings.description: Page 3 header placement is changed in the HTML.
findings.evidence: The PDF places report title/date/page number in the top-right header; the HTML moves this metadata above the section heading in the main flow near the left side.

findings.description: Bullet and heading styling on page 3 are visually altered.
findings.evidence: The PDF uses serif text with underlined/bold topic labels in bullets; the HTML uses sans-serif text with bold labels only. The list remains a list and meaning is preserved.

findings.description: Page 4 running header content is repositioned.
findings.evidence: The PDF shows the report title/date/page number in the top-right margin, while the HTML inserts this metadata mid-flow above the continuation and section III heading.

findings.description: Section III heading and list typography differ from the PDF.
findings.evidence: The PDF uses serif section styling and tighter bullet formatting; the HTML uses larger sans-serif headings and more web-like list spacing. Content and bullet structure are preserved.

findings.description: Page 5 header information is moved into the content flow.
findings.evidence: The PDF has the report title/date/page number in the top-right header; the HTML places this block above the final bullets and before 'IV. Attachments' within the main body area.

findings.description: Attachment list formatting is simplified in HTML.
findings.evidence: The PDF presents the attachment list in serif text with aligned Roman numerals and item labels; the HTML shows a simpler text list with less formal alignment. Items remain distinct and readable.

findings.description: HTML includes fabricated digital-signature block and contact metadata not present in the PDF.
findings.evidence: HTML adds: "Digitally signed by Elizabeth S Werner", "DN: cn=Elizabeth S Werner, o=DWM-Soild Waste, ou=NCDENR, email=elizabeth.werner@ncdenr.gov, c=US", and "Date: 2016.04.28 10:23:05 -04'00'". These lines do not appear anywhere in the provided PDF pages, which show only the From block: "Elizabeth S. Werner / Division of Waste Management / Raleigh Central Office".

findings.description: Heading hierarchy is incomplete because the document header content on later pages is encoded as repeated page-header paragraphs rather than structural headings.
findings.evidence:
PDF uses repeated running header text on pages 2-5: "Meeting Officer’s Report / Coal Ash Impoundment Classification(s) / Roxboro Steam Electric Plant, Person County / April 28, 2016 / Page X of 5". HTML represents these as multiple

...

blocks instead of preserving only the true section headings. While main section headings are mapped to h1/h2 correctly, the structural treatment of repeated header matter is inconsistent.

findings.description: HTML duplicates the main document container with nested <main> elements, creating redundant structure and repeated page-header artifacts.
findings.evidence: HTML contains an outer "<main>" and then another inner "<main>" around the whole document content. In addition, the repeated per-page running headers are preserved as separate blocks before each section, which duplicates document-identification content across the HTML.

findings.description: There are text accuracy issues including OCR/transcription errors and punctuation differences that alter fidelity.
findings.evidence: HTML signature metadata misspells organization name as "DWM-Soild Waste". In body text, HTML has "there were no creditable reports" and "Duke wouldn’t test there wells"; PDF shows the same visible wording for those bullets, so those are not conversion errors. However, HTML also changes spacing/punctuation in places such as "Risk Classification –Some commenters" versus PDF "Risk Classification –Some commenters" visually close, and converts layout-only formatting into prose blocks. The most substantive inaccuracy remains the inserted signature metadata.
